lines.speaker: Aristarchus
lines.text: I'm sorry, but I'm currently doing some research. Could you talk to me later, please?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay then.

lines.speaker: Aristarchus
lines.text: Good day, [madam/sir]. Are you interested in the history of this area?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about yourself.

lines.speaker: Aristarchus
lines.text: I am a librarian from the great library of Menaphos. I left to travel the desert and learn more of its history.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd better be off.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about Enakhra.

lines.speaker: Aristarchus
lines.text: Enakhra? I have read only a little about her. She was one of the Mahjarrat who arrived in Gielinor many thousands of years ago.

lines.speaker: Aristarchus
lines.text: She supported Zamorak when he tried to gain power, and she apparently built a great temple in his honour somewhere in the desert.

lines.speaker: Aristarchus
lines.text: However, it is said that when she showed it to him, he was not impressed, and told her that he would rather have a weapon that could slay gods.

lines.speaker: Aristarchus
lines.text: That is the last anyone knows of what happened to her, and it took place several thousand years ago.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Interesting. Thanks for the information.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about Akthanakos.

lines.speaker: Aristarchus
lines.text: An interesting choice of topic. Akthanakos is a Mahjarrat who was apparently worshipped as a god for a while in these parts. That was a long time ago, of course.

lines.speaker: Aristarchus
lines.text: Unlike many of his kin, he was against Zamorak's rise to power. Perhaps that's why he ended up out here. Zamorak has little sway in the desert.

lines.speaker: Aristarchus
lines.text: Whatever his reasons, he clearly took a liking to living here. He took on the head of a camel and even made an amulet that would allow him to understand their kind.

lines.speaker: Aristarchus
lines.text: Using it, he even trained some of the native camels to fight for him. Their name, 'Ugthanki', is derived from his.

lines.speaker: Aristarchus
lines.text: Since he disappeared, however, it is said that the Ugthanki became more violent and started attacking travellers.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm good.

lines.speaker: Aristarchus
lines.text: Good day, [sir/madam]. Are you interested in the history of this area?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I want to know more about the desert!

lines.speaker: Aristarchus
lines.text: It's good to meet someone so eager to learn.

lines.speaker: Aristarchus
lines.text: Now, what would you like to hear about?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about Enakhra the Mahjarrat.

lines.speaker: Aristarchus
lines.text: I have read only a little about her. She was one of the Mahjarrat who arrived in Gielinor many thousands of years ago.

lines.speaker: Aristarchus
lines.text: She supported Zamorak when he tried to gain power, but her attempts to gain his favour failed, and she retreated to a temple she had built.

lines.speaker: Aristarchus
lines.text: That is the last anyone knows of what happened to her, and it took place several thousand years ago.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about Akthanakos the Mahjarrat.

lines.speaker: Aristarchus
lines.text: Like Enakhra, his rival, Akthanakos came to Gielinor with the other Mahjarrat. He took on the aspect of one of the local gods, and is usually drawn with a camel's head.

lines.speaker: Aristarchus
lines.text: He was against Zamorak's rise to power, but the last anyone knows of him was that he and Enakhra were thinking of forgetting their rivalry.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about Enakhra's temple.

lines.speaker: Aristarchus
lines.text: Well, my sources say that Enakhra tried to gain favour with Zamorak by building him a temple somewhere in the desert.

lines.speaker: Aristarchus
lines.text: It is said that when she showed it to him, he was not impressed, and told her that he would rather have a weapon that could slay gods than a temple.

lines.speaker: Aristarchus
lines.text: After that, it seems that Enakhra retreated to that temple. If she hasn't died, she's been there ever since.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about the Camulet.

lines.speaker: Aristarchus
lines.text: I'm afraid I've only heard rumours of it, but here's what I know.

lines.speaker: Aristarchus
lines.text: When Akthanakos came to Gielinor and took on the aspect of a man with a camel's head, he made an amulet that would allow him to understand camels.

lines.speaker: Aristarchus
lines.text: Using it, he even trained some of the native camels to fight for him. Their name, 'Ugthanki', is derived from his.

lines.speaker: Aristarchus
lines.text: Since he disappeared, however, it is said that the Ugthanki became more violent and started attacking travellers.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why should I care about what happened thousands of years ago?

lines.speaker: Aristarchus
lines.text: Because those who cannot remember the past are condemned to repeat it.

lines.speaker: Aristarchus
lines.text: Knowing something about history can prevent you from making mistakes that people have made before, you see.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who are you?

lines.speaker: Aristarchus
lines.text: I am a librarian from the great library of Menaphos. I left to travel the desert and learn more of its history.

lines.speaker: Aristarchus
lines.text: Unfortunately, the plague in Sophanem prevents me from returning to the library.
